name: icontract

metadata.name: icontract
metadata.summary: Provide design-by-contract with informative violation messages.

documentation:
# icontract

```{image} https://github.com/Parquery/icontract/workflows/CI/badge.svg
:alt: Continuous integration
:target: https://github.com/Parquery/icontract/actions?query=workflow%3ACI
```

```{image} https://coveralls.io/repos/github/Parquery/icontract/badge.svg?branch=master
:target: https://coveralls.io/github/Parquery/icontract
```

```{image} https://badge.fury.io/py/icontract.svg
:alt: PyPI - version
:target: https://badge.fury.io/py/icontract
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/icontract.svg
:alt: PyPI - Python Version
```

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/icontract/badge/?version=latest
:alt: Documentation
:target: https://icontract.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
```

```{image} https://badges.gitter.im/gitterHQ/gitter.svg
:alt: Gitter chat
:target: https://gitter.im/Parquery-icontract/community
```

icontract provides [design-by-contract](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Design_by_contract) to Python3 with informative
violation messages and inheritance.

It also gives a base for a flourishing of a wider ecosystem:

- A linter [pyicontract-lint],

- A sphinx plug-in [sphinx-icontract],

- A tool [icontract-hypothesis] for automated testing and ghostwriting test files which infers
  [Hypothesis] strategies based on the contracts,

  - together with IDE integrations such as
    [icontract-hypothesis-vim],
    [icontract-hypothesis-pycharm], and
    [icontract-hypothesis-vscode],

- Directly integrated into [CrossHair], a tool for automatic verification of Python programs,

  - together with IDE integrations such as
    [crosshair-pycharm] and [crosshair-vscode], and

- An integration with [FastAPI] through [fastapi-icontract] to enforce contracts on your HTTP API and display them
  in OpenAPI 3 schema and Swagger UI, and

- An extensive corpus, [Python-by-contract corpus], of Python programs annotated with contracts for educational, testing and research purposes.

## Related Projects

There exist a couple of contract libraries. However, at the time of this writing (September 2018), they all required the
programmer either to learn a new syntax ([PyContracts](https://pypi.org/project/PyContracts/)) or to write
redundant condition descriptions (
*e.g.*,
[contracts](https://pypi.org/project/contracts/),
[covenant](https://github.com/kisielk/covenant),
[deal](https://github.com/life4/deal),
[dpcontracts](https://pypi.org/project/dpcontracts/),
[pyadbc](https://pypi.org/project/pyadbc/) and
[pcd](https://pypi.org/project/pcd)).

This library was strongly inspired by them, but we go two steps further.

First, our violation message on contract breach are much more informative. The message includes the source code of the
contract condition as well as variable values at the time of the breach. This promotes don't-repeat-yourself principle
([DRY](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Don%27t_repeat_yourself)) and spare the programmer the tedious task of repeating
the message that was already written in code.

Second, icontract allows inheritance of the contracts and supports weakining of the preconditions
as well as strengthening of the postconditions and invariants. Notably, weakining and strengthening of the contracts
is a feature indispensable for modeling many non-trivial class hierarchies. Please see Section
[Inheritance](https://icontract.readthedocs.io/en/latest/usage.html#inheritance).
To the best of our knowledge, there is currently no other Python library that supports inheritance of the contracts in a
correct way.

In the long run, we hope that design-by-contract will be adopted and integrated in the language. Consider this library
a work-around till that happens. You might be also interested in the archived discussion on how to bring
design-by-contract into Python language on
[python-ideas mailing list](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/python-ideas/JtMgpSyODTU).

# Teasers

We give a couple of teasers here to motivate the library.
Please see the documentation available on [icontract.readthedocs.io](https://icontract.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) for a full scope of its
capabilities.

The script is also available as a [repl.it post].

```python
>>> import icontract

>>> @icontract.require(lambda x: x > 3)
... def some_func(x: int, y: int = 5) -> None:
...     pass
...

>>> some_func(x=5)

# Pre-condition violation
>>> some_func(x=1)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
icontract.errors.ViolationError: File <doctest README.rst[1]>, line 1 in <module>:
x > 3:
x was 1
y was 5

# Pre-condition violation with a description
>>> @icontract.require(lambda x: x > 3, "x must not be small")
... def some_func(x: int, y: int = 5) -> None:
...     pass
...
>>> some_func(x=1)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
icontract.errors.ViolationError: File <doctest README.rst[4]>, line 1 in <module>:
x must not be small: x > 3:
x was 1
y was 5

# Pre-condition violation with more complex values
>>> class B:
...     def __init__(self) -> None:
...         self.x = 7
...
...     def y(self) -> int:
...         return 2
...
...     def __repr__(self) -> str:
...         return "instance of B"
...
>>> class A:
...     def __init__(self) -> None:
...         self.b = B()
...
...     def __repr__(self) -> str:
...         return "instance of A"
...
>>> SOME_GLOBAL_VAR = 13
>>> @icontract.require(lambda a: a.b.x + a.b.y() > SOME_GLOBAL_VAR)
... def some_func(a: A) -> None:
...     pass
...
>>> an_a = A()
>>> some_func(an_a)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
icontract.errors.ViolationError: File <doctest README.rst[9]>, line 1 in <module>:
a.b.x + a.b.y() > SOME_GLOBAL_VAR:
SOME_GLOBAL_VAR was 13
a was instance of A
a.b was instance of B
a.b.x was 7
a.b.y() was 2

# Post-condition
>>> @icontract.ensure(lambda result, x: result > x)
... def some_func(x: int, y: int = 5) -> int:
...     return x - y
...
>>> some_func(x=10)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
icontract.errors.ViolationError: File <doctest README.rst[12]>, line 1 in <module>:
result > x:
result was 5
x was 10
y was 5


# Pre-conditions fail before post-conditions.
>>> @icontract.ensure(lambda result, x: result > x)
... @icontract.require(lambda x: x > 3, "x must not be small")
... def some_func(x: int, y: int = 5) -> int:
...    return x - y
...
>>> some_func(x=3)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
icontract.errors.ViolationError: File <doctest README.rst[14]>, line 2 in <module>:
x must not be small: x > 3:
x was 3
y was 5

# Invariant
>>> @icontract.invariant(lambda self: self.x > 0)
... class SomeClass:
...     def __init__(self) -> None:
...         self.x = -1
...
...     def __repr__(self) -> str:
...         return "an instance of SomeClass"
...
>>> some_instance = SomeClass()
Traceback (most recent call last):
 ...
icontract.errors.ViolationError: File <doctest README.rst[16]>, line 1 in <module>:
self.x > 0:
self was an instance of SomeClass
self.x was -1
```

# Installation

- Install icontract with pip:

```bash
pip3 install icontract
```

# Versioning

We follow [Semantic Versioning](http://semver.org/spec/v1.0.0.html). The version X.Y.Z indicates:

- X is the major version (backward-incompatible),
- Y is the minor version (backward-compatible), and
- Z is the patch version (backward-compatible bug fix).

[crosshair]: https://github.com/pschanely/CrossHair
[crosshair-pycharm]: https://github.com/mristin/crosshair-pycharm/
[crosshair-vscode]: https://github.com/mristin/crosshair-vscode/
[fastapi]: https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/issues/1996
[fastapi-icontract]: https://pypi.org/project/fastapi-icontract/
[hypothesis]: https://hypothesis.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
[icontract-hypothesis]: https://github.com/mristin/icontract-hypothesis
[icontract-hypothesis-pycharm]: https://github.com/mristin/icontract-hypothesis-pycharm
[icontract-hypothesis-vim]: https://github.com/mristin/icontract-hypothesis-vim
[icontract-hypothesis-vscode]: https://github.com/mristin/icontract-hypothesis-vscode
[pyicontract-lint]: https://pypi.org/project/pyicontract-lint
[python-by-contract corpus]: https://github.com/mristin/python-by-contract-corpus
[repl.it post]: https://repl.it/talk/share/icontract-example-script/121190
[sphinx-icontract]: https://pypi.org/project/sphinx-icontract

modules.name: icontract.errors
documentation: Define public errors and exceptions.

classes.name: icontract.errors.ViolationError
documentation: Indicate a violation of a contract.

modules.name: icontract
documentation: Decorate functions with contracts.

modules.name: icontract._metaclass
documentation: Define the metaclass necessary to inherit the contracts from the base classes.

functions.name: icontract._metaclass._collapse_invariants
documentation:
Collect invariants from the bases and merge them with the invariants in the namespace.

We do not only collapse `__invariants__` class property, but we also need to collapse
the filtered `__invariants_on_call__` and `__invariants_on_setattr__`, as they are
sub-lists of the `__invariants__`.
Pylint can't deal with multiple Python versions and breaks on ``if``'s on method definitions.
pylint: skip-file

functions.name: icontract._metaclass._collapse_preconditions
documentation:
Collapse function preconditions with the preconditions collected from the base classes.

```
param base_preconditions

preconditions collected from the base classes (grouped by base class)

param bases_have_func

True if one of the base classes has the function

param preconditions

preconditions of the function (before the collapse)

param func

function whose preconditions we are collapsing

return

collapsed sequence of precondition groups
```

functions.name: icontract._metaclass._collapse_snapshots
documentation:
Collapse snapshots of pre-invocation values with the snapshots collected from the base classes.

```
param base_snapshots

snapshots collected from the base classes

param snapshots

snapshots of the function (before the collapse)

return

collapsed sequence of snapshots
```

functions.name: icontract._metaclass._collapse_postconditions
documentation:
Collapse function postconditions with the postconditions collected from the base classes.

```
param base_postconditions

postconditions collected from the base classes

param postconditions

postconditions of the function (before the collapse)

return

collapsed sequence of postconditions
```

functions.name: icontract._metaclass._decorate_namespace_function
documentation: Collect preconditions and postconditions from the bases and decorate the function at the `key`.

functions.name: icontract._metaclass._decorate_namespace_property
documentation: Collect contracts for all getters/setters/deleters corresponding to `key` and decorate them.

functions.name: icontract._metaclass._dbc_decorate_namespace
documentation:
Collect invariants, preconditions and postconditions from the bases and decorate all the methods.

Instance methods are simply replaced with the decorated function/ Properties, class methods and static methods are
overridden with new instances of `property`, `classmethod` and `staticmethod`, respectively.

functions.name: icontract._metaclass._register_for_hypothesis
documentation:
Add `cls` to `_CONTRACT_CLASSES` to be later registered with icontract_hypothesis.

icontract_hypothesis is expected to monkey-patch this function.
Prior to patching, all the classes in `_CONTRACT_CLASSES` should be registered
with Hypothesis.

The registration is necessary so that the preconditions on the \_\_init\_\_ are propagated
in `hypothesis.strategies.builds`.

variables.name: icontract._metaclass._CONTRACT_CLASSES
documentation: type: ignore

variables.name: icontract._metaclass.T
documentation: pylint: disable=invalid-name

classes.name: icontract._metaclass.DBCMeta

methods.name: icontract._metaclass.DBCMeta.__new__
documentation: Create a class with inherited preconditions, postconditions and invariants.
pylint: disable=arguments-differ

methods.name: icontract._metaclass.DBCMeta.__new__
documentation: Create a class with inherited preconditions, postconditions and invariants.

Define a meta class that allows inheritance of the contracts.

The preconditions are weakened ("require else"), while postconditions ("ensure then") and invariants are
strengthened according to the inheritance rules of the design-by-contract.

classes.name: icontract._metaclass.DBC
documentation: Provide a standard way to create a class which can inherit the contracts.

modules.name: icontract._types
documentation: Define data structures shared among the modules.

classes.name: icontract._types.Contract

methods.name: icontract._types.Contract.__init__
documentation:
Initialize.

```
param condition

condition predicate

param description

textual description of the contract

param a_repr

representation instance that defines how the values are represented

param error

if given as a callable, error is expected to accept a subset of function arguments
(e.g., also including result for preconditions, only self for invariants etc.) and return
an exception. The error is called on contract violation and the resulting exception is raised.

Otherwise, it is expected to denote an Exception class which is instantiated with the violation message
and raised on contract violation.

param location

indicate where the contract was defined (e.g., path and line number)
```
Represent a contract to be enforced as a precondition, postcondition or as an invariant.

classes.name: icontract._types.Snapshot

methods.name: icontract._types.Snapshot.__init__
documentation:
Initialize.

```
param capture

function to capture the snapshot accepting a single argument (from a set of arguments
of the original function)

param name

name of the captured variable in OLD that is passed to postconditions

param location

indicate where the snapshot was defined (e.g., path and line number)
```
Define a snapshot of an argument *prior* to the function invocation that is later supplied to a postcondition.

classes.name: icontract._types.InvariantCheckEvent
documentation: Define when an invariant should be checked.

classes.name: icontract._types.Invariant

methods.name: icontract._types.Invariant.__init__
documentation: Initialize with the given values.
NOTE (mristin):
The class ``Invariant`` inherits from ``Contract`` so that we can maintain
the backwards compatibility with the integrators after introducing
the ``check_on`` feature.
Represent a contract which is checked on all or some of the class operations.

modules.name: icontract._decorators
documentation: Define public decorators.

classes.name: icontract._decorators.require

methods.name: icontract._decorators.require.__init__
documentation:
Initialize.

```
param condition

precondition predicate

If the condition returns a coroutine, you must specify the  as
coroutines have side effects and can not be recomputed.

param description

textual description of the precondition

param a_repr

representation instance that defines how the values are represented

param enabled

The decorator is applied only if this argument is set.

Otherwise, the condition check is disabled and there is no run-time overhead.

The default is to always check the condition unless the interpreter runs in optimized mode (-O or
-OO).

param error

The error is expected to denote either:

A callable. error is expected to accept a subset of function arguments and return an exception.
The error is called on contract violation and the resulting exception is raised.

A subclass of BaseException which is instantiated with the violation message and raised
on contract violation.

An instance of BaseException that will be raised with the traceback on contract violation.
```

methods.name: icontract._decorators.require.__call__
documentation:
Add the precondition to the list of preconditions of the function `func`.

The function `func` is decorated with a contract checker if there is no contract checker in
the decorator stack.

```
param func

function to be wrapped

return

contract checker around func if no contract checker on the decorator stack, or func otherwise
```

Decorate a function with a precondition.

The arguments of the precondition are expected to be a subset of the arguments of the wrapped function.

classes.name: icontract._decorators.snapshot

methods.name: icontract._decorators.snapshot.__init__
documentation:
Initialize.

```
param capture

function to capture the snapshot accepting a one or more arguments of the original function prior
to the execution

param name

name of the snapshot; if omitted, the name corresponds to the name of the input argument

param enabled

The decorator is applied only if enabled is set.

Otherwise, the snapshot is disabled and there is no run-time overhead.

The default is to always capture the snapshot unless the interpreter runs in optimized mode (-O or
-OO).
```

methods.name: icontract._decorators.snapshot.__call__
documentation:
Add the snapshot to the list of snapshots of the function `func`.

The function `func` is expected to be decorated with at least one postcondition before the snapshot.

```
param func

function whose arguments we need to snapshot

return

func as given in the input
```

Decorate a function with a snapshot of argument values captured *prior* to the function invocation.

A snapshot is defined by a capture function (usually a lambda) that accepts one or more arguments of the function.
If the name of the snapshot is not given, the capture function must have a single argument and the name is equal to
the name of that single argument.

The captured values are supplied to postconditions with the OLD argument of the condition and error function.
Snapshots are inherited from the base classes and must not have conflicting names in the class hierarchy.

classes.name: icontract._decorators.ensure

methods.name: icontract._decorators.ensure.__init__
documentation:
Initialize.

```
param condition

postcondition predicate.

If the condition returns a coroutine, you must specify the  as
coroutines have side effects and can not be recomputed.

param description

textual description of the postcondition

param a_repr

representation instance that defines how the values are represented

param enabled

The decorator is applied only if this argument is set.

Otherwise, the condition check is disabled and there is no run-time overhead.

The default is to always check the condition unless the interpreter runs in optimized mode (-O or
-OO).

param error

The error is expected to denote either:

A callable. error is expected to accept a subset of function arguments and return an exception.
The error is called on contract violation and the resulting exception is raised.

A subclass of BaseException which is instantiated with the violation message and raised
on contract violation.

An instance of BaseException that will be raised with the traceback on contract violation.
```

methods.name: icontract._decorators.ensure.__call__
documentation:
Add the postcondition to the list of postconditions of the function `func`.

The function `func` is decorated with a contract checker if there is no contract checker in
the decorator stack.

```
param func

function to be wrapped

return

contract checker around func if no contract checker on the decorator stack, or func otherwise
```

Decorate a function with a postcondition.

The arguments of the postcondition are expected to be a subset of the arguments of the wrapped function.
Additionally, the argument "result" is reserved for the result of the wrapped function. The wrapped function must
not have "result" among its arguments.

classes.name: icontract._decorators.invariant

methods.name: icontract._decorators.invariant.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a class decorator to establish the invariant on all the public methods.

```
param condition

invariant predicate.

The condition must not be a coroutine function as dunder functions (including __init__)
of a class can not be async.

param description

textual description of the invariant

param a_repr

representation instance that defines how the values are represented

param enabled

The decorator is applied only if this argument is set.

Otherwise, the condition check is disabled and there is no run-time overhead.

The default is to always check the condition unless the interpreter runs in optimized mode (-O or
-OO).

param error

The error is expected to denote either:

A callable. error is expected to accept a subset of function arguments and return an exception.
The error is called on contract violation and the resulting exception is raised.

A subclass of BaseException which is instantiated with the violation message and raised
on contract violation.

An instance of BaseException that will be raised with the traceback on contract violation.

param check_on

Specify when to check the invariant.

If  is set, the invariant will be checked on all the method calls
except __setattr__.

If  is set, the invariant will be checked on __setattr__ calls.

return

```

methods.name: icontract._decorators.invariant.__call__
documentation:
Decorate each of the public methods with the invariant.

Go through the decorator stack of each function and search for a contract checker. If there is one,
add the invariant to the checker's invariants. If there is no checker in the stack, wrap the function with a
contract checker.

Represent a class decorator to establish the invariant on all the public methods.

Class method as well as "private" (prefix `__`) and "protected" methods (prefix `_`) may violate the invariant.
Note that all magic methods (prefix `__` and suffix `__`) are considered public and hence also need to establish
the invariant. To avoid endless loops when generating the error message on an invariant breach, the method
`__repr__` is deliberately exempt from observing the invariant.

The invariant is checked *before* and *after* the method invocation.

As invariants need to wrap dunder methods, including `__init__`, their conditions *can not* be
async, as most dunder methods need to be synchronous methods, and wrapping them with async code would
break that constraint.

For efficiency reasons, we do not check the invariants at the calls to `__setattr__` and `__getattr__`
methods by default. If you indeed want to check the invariants in those calls as well, make sure to adapt
the argument `check_on` accordingly.

modules.name: icontract._globals
documentation: Define global variables used among the modules.

variables.name: icontract._globals.aRepr
documentation: pylint: disable=invalid-name

modules.name: icontract._represent
documentation: Handle representations necessary for informative error messages.

functions.name: icontract._represent._representable
documentation:
Check whether we want to represent the value in the error message on contract breach.

We do not want to represent classes, methods, modules and functions.

```
param value

value related to an AST node

return

True if we want to represent it in the violation error
```
pylint does not play with typing.Mapping.
pylint: disable=unsubscriptable-object

functions.name: icontract._represent.is_lambda
documentation:
Check whether the function is a lambda function.

```pycon
>>> def some_func()->bool: return True
>>> is_lambda(some_func)
False
```

```pycon
>>> lmbd = lambda x: x > 0
>>> is_lambda(lmbd)
True
```

```
return

True if condition is defined as lambda function
```

functions.name: icontract._represent.inspect_decorator
documentation:
Parse the file in which the decorator is called and figure out the corresponding call AST node.

```
param lines

lines of the source file corresponding to the decorator call

param lineno

line index (starting with 0) of one of the lines in the decorator call

param filename

name of the file where decorator is called

return

inspected decorator call
```

functions.name: icontract._represent.find_lambda_condition
documentation:
Inspect the decorator and extract the condition as lambda.

If the condition is not given as a lambda function, return None.

functions.name: icontract._represent.inspect_lambda_condition
documentation:
Try to extract the source code of the condition as lambda.

If the condition is not a lambda, returns None.

functions.name: icontract._represent.collect_variable_lookup
documentation:
Collect the variable lookups in order of precedence.

```
param condition

contract condition for which we are constructing a variable look-up tables

param resolved_kwargs

keyword arguments of the original function to be passed over to the condition (where applicable).

The keyword arguments are added to the variable look-up tables accordingly.
If resolved_kwargs is None, no keyword arguments will be added to the variable look-up table.
```
fmt: off

functions.name: icontract._represent.repr_values
documentation:
Represent function arguments and frame values in the error message on contract breach.

```
param condition

condition function of the contract

param lambda_inspection

inspected lambda AST node corresponding to the condition function (None if the condition was not given as a
lambda function)

param resolved_kwargs

arguments put in the function call

param a_repr

representation instance that defines how the values are represented.

return

list of value representations
```

functions.name: icontract._represent.represent_condition
documentation: Represent the condition as a string.

functions.name: icontract._represent.generate_message
documentation: Generate the message upon contract violation.

classes.name: icontract._represent.Visitor

methods.name: icontract._represent.Visitor.__init__
documentation:
Initialize.

```
param recomputed_values

AST node of a condition function -> value associated with the node

param variable_lookup

list of lookup tables to look-up the values of the variables, sorted by precedence.
The visitor needs it here to check whether we overrode a built-in variable (like id).

param atok

parsed AST tree and tokens with additional positions in source code
```
pylint: disable=invalid-name
pylint: disable=missing-docstring

methods.name: icontract._represent.Visitor.visit_JoinedStr
documentation: Show the whole joined strings without descending into the values.

methods.name: icontract._represent.Visitor.visit_Name
documentation:
Resolve the name from the variable look-up and the built-ins.

Due to possible branching (e.g., If-expressions), some nodes might lack the recomputed values. These nodes
are ignored.

methods.name: icontract._represent.Visitor.visit_Attribute
documentation: Represent the attribute by dumping its source code.

methods.name: icontract._represent.Visitor.visit_NamedExpr
documentation: Represent the target with the value of the node.

methods.name: icontract._represent.Visitor.visit_Call
documentation: Represent the call by dumping its source code.

methods.name: icontract._represent.Visitor.visit_ListComp
documentation: Represent the list comprehension by dumping its source code.

methods.name: icontract._represent.Visitor.visit_SetComp
documentation: Represent the set comprehension by dumping its source code.

methods.name: icontract._represent.Visitor.visit_DictComp
documentation: Represent the dictionary comprehension by dumping its source code.

methods.name: icontract._represent.Visitor.visit_Subscript
documentation: Represent the subscript with its source code.
Traverse the abstract syntax tree and collect the representations of the selected nodes.

classes.name: icontract._represent.ConditionLambdaInspection

documentation:
Initialize.

```
param atok

parsed AST tree and tokens with added positional properties

param node

lambda AST node corresponding to the condition
```
Represent the inspection of the condition function given as a lambda.

classes.name: icontract._represent.DecoratorInspection

documentation:
Initialize.

```
param atok

parsed AST tree and tokens with added positional properties

param node

lambda AST node corresponding to the condition
```
Represent the inspection of a decorator extracted from a source file and embedded in a dummy dynamic module.

modules.name: icontract._recompute
documentation: Handle re-computation of values of a function given its abstract syntax tree and function frame.

functions.name: icontract._recompute._translate_all_expression_to_a_module
documentation:
Generate the AST of the module to trace an all quantifier on an generator expression.

```
param generator_exp

generator expression to be translated

param generated_function_name

UUID of the tracing function to be used in the code

param name_to_value

mapping of all resolved values to the variable names
(passed as arguments to the function so that the generation can access them)

return

translation to a module
```
noinspection PyTypeChecker

classes.name: icontract._recompute.Placeholder

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Placeholder.__repr__
documentation: Represent the placeholder as <Placeholder>.
Represent a placeholder for variables local to the lambda such as targets in generator expressions.

classes.name: icontract._recompute.FirstExceptionInAll

documentation:
Initialize with the given values.

```
param result

value of the evaluation which was not truthy

param inputs

all the target loop variables set during the iteration
```

methods.name: icontract._recompute.FirstExceptionInAll.__bool__
documentation: Return the result of the ELT evaluation which invalidated the `all` quantifier.
Represent a first exception case for which an all quantifier does not apply.

classes.name: icontract._recompute._CollectStoredNamesVisitor

methods.name: icontract._recompute._CollectStoredNamesVisitor.visit_Name
documentation: Collect the name if it is in a store context.
Traverse the abstract syntax tree and collect all the names which are stored.

classes.name: icontract._recompute._CollectNameLoadsVisitor

methods.name: icontract._recompute._CollectNameLoadsVisitor.visit_Name
documentation: Collect the name if it is in a load context.
Traverse the abstract syntax tree and collect all the name nodes in Load context.

classes.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.__init__
documentation:
Initialize.

```
param variable_lookup

list of lookup tables to look-up the values of the variables, sorted by precedence
```
pylint: disable=invalid-name
pylint: disable=missing-docstring

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_Num
documentation: Recompute the value as the number at the node.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_Str
documentation: Recompute the value as the string at the node.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_Bytes
documentation: Recompute the value as the bytes at the node.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_NameConstant
documentation: Forward the node value as a result.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_Constant
documentation: Forward the node value as a result.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_FormattedValue
documentation: Format the node value.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_JoinedStr
documentation: Visit the values and concatenate them.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_List
documentation: Visit the elements and assemble the results into a list.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_Tuple
documentation: Visit the elements and assemble the results into a tuple.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_Set
documentation: Visit the elements and assemble the results into a set.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_Dict
documentation: Visit keys and values and assemble a dictionary with the results.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_Name
documentation: Load the variable by looking it up in the variable look-up and in the built-ins.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_Expr
documentation: Visit the node's `value`.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_UnaryOp
documentation: Visit the node operand and apply the operation on the result.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_BinOp
documentation: Recursively visit the left and right operand, respectively, and apply the operation on the results.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_BoolOp
documentation: Recursively visit the operands and apply the operation on them.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_Compare
documentation: Recursively visit the comparators and apply the operations on them.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_Call
documentation: Visit the function and the arguments and finally make the function call with them.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_IfExp
documentation: Visit the `test`, and depending on its outcome, the `body` or `orelse`.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_Attribute
documentation: Visit the node's `value` and get the attribute from the result.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_NamedExpr
documentation: Visit the node's `value` and assign it to both this node and the target.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_Index
documentation: Visit the node's `value`.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_Slice
documentation: Visit `lower`, `upper` and `step` and recompute the node as a `slice`.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_ExtSlice
documentation: Visit each dimension of the advanced slicing and assemble the dimensions in a tuple.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_Subscript
documentation: Visit the `slice` and a `value` and get the element.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor._trace_all_with_generator
documentation: Re-write the all call with for loops to trace the first offending item, if any.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor._execute_comprehension
documentation: Compile the generator or comprehension from the node and execute the compiled code.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_GeneratorExp
documentation: Compile the generator expression as a function and call it.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_ListComp
documentation: Compile the list comprehension as a function and call it.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_SetComp
documentation: Compile the set comprehension as a function and call it.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_DictComp
documentation: Compile the dictionary comprehension as a function and call it.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_Lambda
documentation: Do not support inline lambda until there is a feature request since this is quite tricky to implement.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.visit_Return
documentation: Raise an exception that this node is unexpected.

methods.name: icontract._recompute.Visitor.generic_visit
documentation: Raise an exception that this node has not been handled.

Traverse the abstract syntax tree and recompute the values of each node defined by the function frame.

```
ivar recomputed_values

mapping node -> value assigned to each visited node

type recomputed_values

Mapping[ast.AST, Any]
```
noinspection PyTypeChecker

modules.name: icontract._checkers
documentation: Provide functions to add/find contract checkers.

functions.name: icontract._checkers._walk_decorator_stack
documentation:
Iterate through the stack of decorated functions until the original function.

Assume that all decorators used functools.update_wrapper.
pylint does not play with typing.Mapping.
pylint: disable=unsubscriptable-object
pylint: disable=raising-bad-type

functions.name: icontract._checkers.find_checker
documentation: Iterate through the decorator stack till we find the contract checker.

functions.name: icontract._checkers.kwargs_from_call
documentation:
Inspect the input values received at the wrapper for the actual function call.

```
param param_names

parameter (i.e. argument) names of the original (decorated) function

param kwdefaults

default argument values of the original function

param args

arguments supplied to the call

param kwargs

keyword arguments supplied to the call

return

resolved arguments as they would be passed to the function
```

functions.name: icontract._checkers.not_check
documentation:
Negate the check value of a condition and capture missing boolyness (*e.g.*, when check is a numpy array).

```
param check

value of the evaluated condition

param contract

corresponding to the check

return

negated check

raise

ValueError if the check could not be negated
```

functions.name: icontract._checkers.select_condition_kwargs
documentation:
Select the keyword arguments that are used by the contract.

```
param contract

contract to be verified

param resolved_kwargs

resolved keyword arguments of the call (including the default argument values of the decorated function)

return

a subset of resolved_kwargs
```

functions.name: icontract._checkers._assert_no_invalid_kwargs
documentation: Check that kwargs of a function contain no unexpected arguments.

functions.name: icontract._checkers._unpack_pre_snap_posts
documentation: Retrieve the preconditions, snapshots and postconditions defined for the given wrapper checker.

functions.name: icontract._checkers._assert_resolved_kwargs_valid
documentation: Check that the resolved kwargs of a decorated function are valid.

functions.name: icontract._checkers._create_violation_error
documentation: Create the violation error based on the violated contract.

functions.name: icontract._checkers._assert_preconditions_async
documentation: Assert that the preconditions of an async function hold.

functions.name: icontract._checkers._assert_preconditions
documentation: Assert that the preconditions of a sync function hold.

functions.name: icontract._checkers._capture_old_async
documentation: Capture all snapshots of an async function and return the captured values bundled in an `Old`.

functions.name: icontract._checkers._capture_old
documentation: Capture all snapshots of a sync function and return the captured values bundled in an `Old`.

functions.name: icontract._checkers._assert_postconditions_async
documentation: Assert that the postconditions of an async function hold.

functions.name: icontract._checkers._assert_postconditions
documentation: Assert that the postconditions of a sync function hold.

functions.name: icontract._checkers._assert_invariant
documentation: Assert that the contract holds as a class invariant given the instance of the class.

functions.name: icontract._checkers.select_capture_kwargs
documentation:
Select the keyword arguments that are used by the snapshot capture.

```
param a_snapshot

snapshot to be captured

param resolved_kwargs

resolved keyword arguments (including the default values)

return

a subset of resolved_kwargs
```

functions.name: icontract._checkers.select_error_kwargs
documentation:
Select the keyword arguments that are used by the error creator of the contract.

```
param contract

contract that was violated and for which we want to generate an error

param resolved_kwargs

resolved keyword arguments (including the default values)

return

a subset of resolved_kwargs
```

functions.name: icontract._checkers.resolve_kwdefaults
documentation: Resolve default values for the function arguments based on its signature.

functions.name: icontract._checkers.decorate_with_checker
documentation: Decorate the function with a checker that verifies the preconditions and postconditions.

functions.name: icontract._checkers.add_precondition_to_checker
documentation:
Add the precondition to the function's checker.

Use {func}`find_checker` to find the checker.
If it returns `None`, decorate it with the checker first using {func}`decorate_with_checker`.

functions.name: icontract._checkers.add_snapshot_to_checker
documentation:
Add the snapshot to the function's checker.

Use {func}`find_checker` to find the checker.
If it returns `None`, decorate it with the checker first using {func}`decorate_with_checker`.

functions.name: icontract._checkers.add_postcondition_to_checker
documentation:
Add the postcondition to the function's checker.

Use {func}`find_checker` to find the checker.
If it returns `None`, decorate it with the checker first using {func}`decorate_with_checker`.

functions.name: icontract._checkers._find_self
documentation: Find the instance of `self` in the arguments.

functions.name: icontract._checkers._decorate_new_with_invariants
documentation:
Decorate the `__new__` of a class s.t. the invariants are checked on the result.

This is necessary for optimized classes such as `namedtuple` which use `object.__init__`
as constructor and do not expect a wrapping around the constructor.

functions.name: icontract._checkers._decorate_with_invariants
documentation:
Decorate the method `func` with invariant checks.

If the function has been already decorated with invariant checks, the function returns immediately.

```
param func

function to be wrapped

param is_init

True if the func is __init__

return

function wrapped with invariant checks
```

functions.name: icontract._checkers._already_decorated_with_invariants
documentation: Check if the function has been already decorated with an invariant check by going through its decorator stack.

functions.name: icontract._checkers.add_invariant_checks
documentation: Decorate each of the class functions with invariant checks if not already decorated.

variables.name: icontract._checkers._IN_PROGRESS
documentation: type: contextvars.ContextVar[Optional[Set[int]]]

variables.name: icontract._checkers._SLOT_WRAPPER_TYPE
documentation: pylint: disable=invalid-name

classes.name: icontract._checkers.Old

methods.name: icontract._checkers.Old.__init__
documentation: Update the `__dict__` with the given mapping.

methods.name: icontract._checkers.Old.__getattr__
documentation: Raise an error as this `item` should not be in the `__dict__`.

methods.name: icontract._checkers.Old.__repr__
documentation: Represent the old values with a string literal as user is unaware of the class.

Represent argument values before the function invocation.

Recipe taken from http://code.activestate.com/recipes/52308-the-simple-but-handy-collector-of-a-bunch-of-named/

classes.name: icontract._checkers._DummyClass
documentation: Represent a dummy class so that we can infer the type of the slot wrapper.
